what_it_does: Implements a reusable, data-driven substrate instrument that renders a wayfinder.json registry as a styled screen, wires a live address filter, and dispatches wayfinder:select events when address elements are tapped.
observations: Defines four small HTML helper functions (chip, tag, addrRow, section) before the main renderer.
DNA.renderWayfinder builds a .screen.wf DOM tree from supplied or fetched wayfinder.json data.
Uses only CSS token classes (wf-*, sbar, screen) and no inline bespoke styling values.
Implements live text filtering by matching against a precomputed data-text attribute on rows.
Collapses sections when all their rows are hidden by the filter.
Dispatches a CustomEvent named wayfinder:select carrying the tapped data-address.
Provides a mount helper that fetches /registry/wayfinder.json, degrades gracefully on failure, and injects the view into a container.
standouts: The file is framed as a 'SUBSTRATE INSTRUMENT' — a reusable archetype rather than a one-off view.
Filtering logic is simple but complete: per-row match plus section visibility.
Event dispatch is the only drill seam; it does not navigate or route internally.
conventions: Follows the DNA namespace pattern: (global.DNA = global.DNA || {}).
Uses an injected esc utility or a local fallback for HTML escaping.
Uses template-literal HTML composition with data-* attributes for behavior hooks.
Prefers CSS class hooks over inline styles.
Uses an IIFE with a global parameter for module isolation.
concerns: esc fallback only escapes &, <, >, ", not single quotes or backticks, which could matter in attribute contexts.
mountWayfinder silently swallows fetch errors and substitutes an empty placeholder; callers get no error signal.
bindWayfinder captures the row list at bind time; rows added later would not be filtered.
The find and click listeners are not unbound, so repeated mounts could accumulate listeners if the old element is not removed from the DOM.
notes: A self-contained view renderer for the wayfinder registry with a single event seam and a live filter; keep an eye on listener cleanup if remounted repeatedly.
questions: Is the esc fallback intentionally minimal, or should it also escape single quotes and backticks for attribute safety?
Should mountWayfinder expose fetch errors to the caller instead of silently degrading?
Is the intended consumer of wayfinder:select expected to handle routing and navigation entirely externally?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to surface any project's wayfinder.json as a reusable, dynamic address-map screen; the code matches that purpose, providing render, bind, and mount helpers and a tap-to-select seam.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create a generic, portable 'address map' instrument that can be dropped into any project consuming a wayfinder registry, with styling and behavior entirely derived from shared DNA tokens.
novelty: It treats a project registry as a navigable identity surface and packages it as a reusable DNA archetype rather than a project-specific page.
contribution: A compact, reusable renderer and interaction binder for wayfinder registries, plus a mount helper with graceful degradation, that a fused system could reuse for any project needing a browsable address map with live filtering and address-selection events.
summary_for_embedding: wayfinder-view.js is a reusable DNA substrate instrument that renders a wayfinder.json registry as a styled screen, provides live address filtering, and emits wayfinder:select events for external drill handling.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to surface any project's address map as a portable, token-styled view component with minimal coupling to the rest of the system.

symbols.does: Renders a small metadata chip showing a numeric or textual value paired with a label.
symbols.description: chip(n, l) returns an HTML span with class wf-chip containing a bold value and a label; it returns an empty string when the value is null or undefined, making it safe for optional meta fields.

symbols.does: Renders a clickable address tag with a data-address attribute.
symbols.description: tag(a) returns a span with class wf-tag whose text and data-address attribute are both set to the escaped address string; these tags are used inside concern rows to represent grouped addresses.

symbols.does: Renders a filterable row for a single address with optional subtitle and meta text.
symbols.description: addrRow(a, sub, meta) builds a .wf-row div carrying a data-address and a lowercase data-text attribute for filtering, with columns for the address, an optional summary, and optional type or reference metadata.

symbols.does: Renders a collapsible wayfinder section with a titled header and optional count badge.
symbols.description: section(title, count, bodyHtml) wraps a block of rows in a .wf-sec container with a .wf-sh header that shows the section title and an optional count, used to group concerns, spine entries, locations, and ghosts.
